title: buckollia volubilis
intro: Buckollia volubilis, also known as the climbing buckwheat or buckwheat vine, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. Traditionally, Buckollia volubilis has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating cough and cold symptoms, and supporting the immune system. Buckollia volubilis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
